title: Chicken Stock / Broth
description: Deep, slow-simmered chicken stock that builds real flavor. This is the backbone of soups, sauces, and anything that actually tastes like it was made with intention.
ingredients: 3 lbs bony chicken pieces (legs, wings, necks or back bones)
3 celery ribs with leaves, cut into chunks
1 tbsp dried rosemary or 2 sprigs fresh rosemary
1 tbsp dried thyme or 2 sprigs fresh thyme
instructions: Place all ingredients in a large pot and add the cold water.
Slowly bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a gentle simmer.
Simmer uncovered for 3 hours, skimming foam as needed.
Remove chicken pieces and let cool until safe to handle.
Pull meat from the bones and set aside for another use. Discard bones.
Strain the broth, discarding vegetables and seasonings.
Skim fat from the surface before using.
notes: Use leftover meat for soups, salads, or sandwiches.
Roasting the bones first will give a deeper, richer flavor.
Freeze in portions so you always have stock ready to go.
whyThisHits: This is the foundation move. Once you start making your own stock, everything else levels up. Soups taste deeper, sauces hit harder, and nothing feels store-bought anymore.
pairing: Use as a base for soups, risottos, sauces, or just sip it hot with a little salt if you are keeping it simple.
footerLine: Art for Your Mouth. Because boring food is a waste of time.
